Florida.script:
The caption/transcript provided appears to be song lyrics from "Iris" by the Goo Goo Dolls, not actual real estate content. There are no talking points, data, or real estate arguments to base a rewrite on, and the location listed as "Tennessee, Tennessee" is not a specific city or market I can add genuine local data to.

To write you a script that actually converts, I need a couple of things from you.

First, what city or metro area in Tennessee is this agent working in? Nashville, Knoxville, Chattanooga, Memphis, and smaller markets all have very different stories to tell.

Second, what is the actual topic or angle you want the script to cover? For example, flood zones, undervalued neighborhoods, insurance costs, new construction deals, investor tips, or something else entirely.

Once I have those two things I can build you a fully conversion-optimized script with real local details, specific numbers, and a CTA that actually starts conversations.

Florida.caption:
Big shift in real estate right now…

Some new listings may show up on one platform before another — which means buyers and sellers aren’t all seeing the same homes at the same time.

Here’s why that matters 👇

• Zillow has significantly more traffic than Redfin
• Less exposure early = fewer buyers seeing your home
• Fewer buyers = potentially less competition

That doesn’t mean it’s always bad.

Some sellers may prefer:
✔️ More privacy
✔️ Controlled rollout
✔️ Testing the market first

But if your goal is to get the strongest offer possible, you need to understand how your home is being marketed.

📌 If you’re selling, ask your agent:
	•	Will my home be on all major platforms right away?
	•	Am I being listed publicly or privately first?
	•	Do I have the option to go fully public?

📌 If you’re buying:
Don’t rely on just one app anymore. Check both Zillow and Redfin so you don’t miss new listings.

At the end of the day…
Exposure matters.

👉 Follow for real estate and homeownership tips
#realestate #homeowners #housingmarket

Florida.title: Big shift in real estate right now… Some new listings may ...
Florida.transcript: Americans, there's a huge war going on in real estate and it's changing how you buy and sell a home that you gotta know about. Huge battle between Zillow and a big real estate brokerage, which ain't the deal on March 16 to push their new listings through Redfin First. That means some coming soon and new listings will show up on Redfin privately first before Zillow. Pros and cons, pros that listing is more private, more exclusivity, the cons, there's only 70 million eyeballs a month on Redfin and 250 million on Zillow. So if you list without brokerage, that's less eyeballs seeing your property right away, so be patient before you save an offer. Also if you're buying a home now you have to check on Redfin and the Zillow app if you want to see all coming soon. Let me know your thoughts below, is this a good thing or bad thing for real estate also which app you like more of myself? I like Redfin. But for those of you guys who are buying a home today, here is the nationwide average mortgage interest rates. Remember these rates are just a ballpark, give your lender a call so you can get an accurate quote.
Florida.script: Something big just changed in real estate and if you're buying or selling in Lehigh Acres right now, you need to know this. Zillow and a major brokerage are in a full-blown battle over new listings. Starting March 16th, some coming soon homes are showing up on Redfin first, privately, before they ever hit Zillow. Here's why that matters to you. Zillow gets 250 million visitors a month. Redfin gets 70 million. So if you're selling a home in Lehigh Acres and your agent only pushes to one platform, you could be missing out on millions of potential buyers from day one. And if you're buying, you now need to check both apps or you're not seeing everything. This is a real shift happening right now. So tell me, are you currently buying or selling in Lehigh Acres? Drop it below and I'll tell you exactly how this affects your situation.

Florida.caption:
Welp, on today’s episode of “Is this really my life?”

The sellers crashed our home inspection & decided it was the perfect time for a shower. 🧼 😭

Real Estate is SO FUN & there is NEVER a dull moment but learning to adapt in ANY environment and pivot under pressure is truly what sets me apart.

Today, I smiled. We pivoted & Thankfully we had a great inspection. 

Bigggg THANK YOU to the 🐐 @essinpectsvalue for your PATIENCE & your attention to detail. 

I can’t wait to see what the next inspection brings us 😂

#realtor #tampabay #realestate #inspector #realtorlife

Florida.fullName: Hannah-Lynn Wallace, YOUR FRIENDLY NEIGHBORHOOD REALTOR®
Florida.title: Welp, on today's episode of “Is this really my life?” The ...
Florida.transcript: Been, been, been, been, been, been, been�
Florida.script: The sellers showed up to their own home inspection and decided it was the perfect time to take a shower. I am not kidding. This actually happened to us in Fort Myers last week. And here is the thing, in this market, you will see everything. Inspections in Fort Myers right now are turning up real surprises, roof damage from Ian that never got properly permitted, AC systems running 10 years past their lifespan, pool equipment that hasn't been touched since 2020. The buyers who come out ahead are the ones with an agent who doesn't panic, pivots fast, and knows exactly what to flag before you're locked in. I've closed 40 plus deals in Lee County and I have seen it all. So here's my question for you. Are you buying in Fort Myers this year, and do you actually know what to look for at your inspection? Drop your timeline below and I'll send you my personal inspection checklist, free.

Florida.caption:
Being a realtor isn’t just one role… it’s a dozen hats 🎩 we wear. 

One moment I’m helping you navigate pricing 📊
Next, I’m finding the perfect home ✨
Then I’m negotiating to get your offer accepted 🤝

And in between…
I’m hearing “I love it” ❤️ and “I hate it” 😅 in the same hour
While adjusting schedules to meet anytime, anywhere ⏱️

It’s about people, emotions, decisions, and timing. It’s about deeply listening and understanding your needs so that I can better serve you.

That’s the role I show up for every single day.

ABC: Always Be Celebrating 🥂

#AdamBrawerEstates #AlwaysBeCelebrating #LosAngelesRealtor #LuxuryRealEstate #RealtorLife

Florida.author: adambrawer
Florida.title: Being a realtor isn't just one role… it's a dozen hats we wear ...
Florida.script: Being a realtor in Punta Gorda isn't just one job. On Monday I'm a negotiator fighting to get my buyer $15K off a home on Shreve Street. Tuesday I'm a flood zone analyst pulling FEMA maps for a waterfront property in Punta Gorda Isles. Wednesday I'm a therapist talking a seller off the ledge because their first offer fell through. Thursday I'm a contractor, walking a home on Marion Avenue flagging $8,000 in repairs the inspector missed. Friday I'm a data analyst tracking why 3 beds under $350K are moving in under 9 days right now. And Saturday I'm doing it all over again at an open house. The right agent in this market isn't just a door opener. Are you working with someone who wears all of these hats, or are you figuring it out alone? Drop your situation below and I'll tell you exactly what you need right now.

Another big milestone for this family as they celebrate their new home in Florida! 🏡✨ A moment that reflects hard work, goals achieved, and exciting new opportunities in their homeownership journey. Congratulations on this amazing accomplishment!

Special recognition to Mernela and Karla for their outstanding teamwork throughout the real estate process 🙌🏼🔑. Successful closing and happy clients! 🌟

USA.caption:
🚨My goal has always been to serve others that’s the foundation of everything I do, and it always will be.

🚨So I built a system that works harder for you than the average agent ever will and I want you to understand what that actually means.

I’ve seen a few comments lately questioning the quality of my reach and following, so let me be transparent. This community is made up of real estate agents from around the world, local buyers, and out-of-state buyers and that is exactly who you want seeing your home. Not random faces. Not empty numbers. People who are either in the market or in the business. That’s the reach that creates real urgency for my sellers! ✨

God gave me this platform. He built it, and Im building it with purpose.
Before your home ever hits the market, buyers already know it’s coming. That’s not luck. That’s strategy.

🚨I’m not only a listing agent either if you’re looking for your next home, I would be so honored to be in your corner for that too.

So when you see my content come across your feed, just know what’s behind it. 14-hour days. Late night calls. Every ounce of energy I have going into the best possible outcome for you and your family.

This isn’t just what I do. It’s what I was called to do. 🤍

#fyp #foryou #foryoupage #realtor #realestate
USA.author: _k.elsie
USA.title: 🚨My goal has always been to serve others that’s the ...
USA.transcript: My goal has always been to serve others. That's the foundation of everything I do as a real certificate. Yes, after I content, you see me show up online consistently, but that is purely intentional for my clients. God has blessed me with this platform, a community full of real estate agents around the world, buyers locally and out of state. And what that means for my clients is real urgency. Before listing Evergates to Market, buyers are you know it's coming. I'll build this to serve you to glorify God and to create a system that will work harder for you than the average agent in the world. So when these posts come across your feed, distance, behind every single one are 14 hour days, light not phone calls, and me pouring everything I have into this best possible strategy for you and your family.
USA.script: Here is something most buyers in Tennessee never think about until it's too late. The agent you choose determines whether you even hear about a home before it hits the market. I spend 14 hours a day building relationships, pulling data, and calling sellers before their sign goes in the ground. That is not an exaggeration. Last month alone, three of my clients got early access to homes in Middle Tennessee before they were ever listed publicly. One of them saved 11 days of competition and came in clean with no bidding war. The average agent is waiting for Zillow to update. I am not that agent. So here is my question for you. Are you planning to buy in Tennessee in the next 6 months or are you still in the research phase? Drop your answer below and I will send you exactly what I am seeing in your target area this week.
